title: Fetal alcohol spectrum disorder: For health professionals - Canada.ca
content:
## Fetal alcohol spectrum disorder: For health professionals
- About, causes and co-occurring conditions
- Supports and services
- For health professionals
- What Canada is doing

## Key publications and reports
These resources are for health and allied professionals interested in more information about fetal alcohol spectrum disorder.
- Canadian Medical Association Journal: 'Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder: A Guideline for Diagnosis Across the Lifespan'
- Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder (FASD)       Prevention: Canadian Perspectives
- Canadian       Guideline for the Clinical Management of High-Risk Drinking and Alcohol       Use Disorder
- Family-centred maternity and newborn care:       National guidelines - Chapter 2: Preconception care
- Family-centred maternity and newborn care:       National guidelines - Chapter 3: Care during pregnancy
- At-a-glance - FASD prevalence among children and youth: results       from the 2019 Canadian Health Survey on Children and Youth
- Population-based prevalence of fetal alcohol       spectrum disorder in Canada
- Teacher-Reported Prevalence of       FASD in Kindergarten in Canada: Association with Child Development and       Problems at Home
- Prevalence of fetal alcohol       spectrum disorder among special subpopulations: a systematic review and       meta-analysis
- Incidence and Prevalence of Fetal Alcohol       Spectrum Disorder by Sex and Age Group in Alberta, Canada
- Kids Brain Health Network
- Canadian Paediatric Society
- FASD and the Justice System
- Centre for Addiction and Mental Health
- The Society of Obstetricians and Gynaecologists of Canada: Alcohol and Pregnancy
- The Lancet: 'Comorbidity of Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis'
